message.content:
Adversaries use Software Discovery to identify installed software and versions on a system or in a cloud environment. This information helps shape subsequent actions, such as determining the presence of security measures or identifying vulnerable software for privilege escalation. Common methods include querying the registry, using enumeration tools, and leveraging malware capabilities to list installed applications and browser versions. Notable examples include Bazar, BRONZE BUTLER, Bundlore, and Cobalt Strike, among others.

The potential impact includes enabling adversaries to tailor their attacks more effectively, increasing the likelihood of successful exploitation and persistence. Adversaries may also use this information to avoid detection by understanding the security landscape of the target environment.

Mitigation strategies involve implementing robust endpoint detection and response (EDR) solutions to monitor and alert on suspicious software enumeration activities. Regularly updating and patching software can reduce the risk of exploitation. Employing least privilege principles and network segmentation can limit the adversary's ability to gather comprehensive software information. Additionally, user training and awareness can help in recognizing and reporting unusual system behaviors indicative of software discovery attempts.
